$schema_note: Sprint brief for the Sanders Intelligence app. Generated by the sprint-planner skill after an In Progress audit + live QA run on 2026-06-16.
theme: Stabilize the data-heavy pages: fix the QA-discovered Inbound truncation and renderer-freeze bugs, lock them in with tests, then ship the PO link, the day-over-day (DoD) breakdown, and the new executive North Star page including its Monthly Star sales-goal section.

project.name: Sanders intelligence app
project.stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite, Tailwind, React Router v6, TanStack Query v5, Supabase, Recharts

selection_rationale: The 2026-06-16 live QA surfaced two high-severity defects on data-heavy pages: Inbound Pipeline silently truncating at 1,000 PO lines, and Inventory Browser / Purchase Orders / Vendor View freezing the renderer for 30-60s+. Those are fixed first because they block the two MVP verification tasks still in progress (1214539638231259, 1214539638231255). The InboundPipeline helper extraction + tests and the exportCsv tests are pulled in alongside because they directly harden the same money-and-data paths the bugs touched. The two user-requested features (PO link on Inbound, day-over-day DoD breakdown) ride on top once the pages are stable. Deferred: cost-increase alerts, mobile responsiveness, and all Phase 3 work. Added 2026-06-16 per user request: the executive North Star page (Asana 1215784652029226), scoped from its description plus two attachments - a design mockup (image.png) and a layout/tech-doc template (BPR-template.html) - both of which the implementer must follow. Added 2026-06-17 per user request: the Monthly Star sales-goal/progress section of the North Star page (Asana 1215798685000043), which depends on the North Star page (task 7).
qa_findings_addressed: BUG 1215774347647240 - Inbound Pipeline truncates at 1,000 PO lines (no pagination).
BUG 1215774057341344 - data-heavy pages freeze the renderer 30-60s+.
in_progress_carryover: 1214539638231259 - MVP Inbound arrival-month/ETA verification: stays In Progress, re-verify after task 1 + 3 land.
1214539638231255 - MVP QA pass error/empty/loading states: stays In Progress, re-verify after task 2 lands.
instructions_for_implementer: Work tasks in the 'order' field. For EACH task: read 'clarifying_questions' first and ask the user before writing code. Treat 'likely_files' as starting points, not the full surface. Implement against 'requirements', validate against 'completion_criteria', and write/extend tests per 'test_coverage'. When done, update the matching Asana task and leave a comment citing file/line evidence.

tasks.title: BUG: Inbound Pipeline silently truncates at 1,000 PO lines (missing pagination)
tasks.outline: Inbound Pipeline only loads the first 1,000 open PO line items because the query is unpaginated, so every KPI (Open Units, Overdue Units, Open Value) and the delivery-month chart undercount. Purchasing makes ordering decisions off these numbers, so the page is actively misleading until this is fixed.
tasks.technical_description: usePOInboundItems() in app/src/hooks/usePurchaseOrders.ts runs a single supabase.from('po_items').select(...).eq(...).gt(...).order(...) with no .range()/.limit(), hitting Supabase's 1000-row default. Replace with the loop-and-range pagination pattern already proven in useInventory.ts (PAGE_SIZE=1000, .range(from, from+PAGE_SIZE-1), break when a short page returns). Preserve the existing inner-join on purchase_orders and the is_active/qty_units_open filters and the expected_delivery_date ordering.
likely_files: app/src/hooks/usePurchaseOrders.ts:55-85 - usePOInboundItems(), the unpaginated query to replace
app/src/hooks/useInventory.ts:139-154 - canonical PAGE_SIZE/.range loop to mirror
app/src/pages/purchasing/InboundPipeline.tsx:106,262 - consumes inbound[]; verify KPI/chart counts rise after the fix
requirements: Paginate po_items past the 1000-row cap so all open PO line items load.
Keep the purchase_orders inner join, is_active=true filter, qty_units_open>0 filter, and expected_delivery_date ordering intact.
No console errors and no UI regression on the Inbound page.
completion_criteria: Open PO Lines KPI shows the true total (greater than 1,000 if more exist) rather than capping at exactly 1,000.
Open Units, Overdue Units, and Open Value recompute over the full set.
A unit/integration test asserts pagination fetches more than one page.
expected_outcomes: Inbound totals and the delivery-month chart reflect every open PO line, so purchasing sees accurate inbound exposure.

unit: usePurchaseOrders test: mock a Supabase response of exactly 1000 then a partial page; assert both pages are concatenated.
integration: Render InboundPipeline with a >1000-row fixture; assert Open PO Lines total exceeds 1000.
manual_qa: Open /purchasing/inbound as admin; confirm Open PO Lines no longer reads 1,000 / 1,000 total and totals look complete.

clarifying_questions: Is loading all open PO lines acceptable for page weight, or should the table move to server-side paging while KPIs still aggregate the full set?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying question and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: BUG: Data-heavy pages freeze the renderer for 30-60s+ (Inventory Browser, Purchase Orders, Vendor View)
tasks.outline: Inventory Browser, Purchase Orders, and Vendor View freeze the browser tab for 30-60s+ on load (sometimes unrecoverable), against a ~12,811-row dataset. Three core purchasing/exec pages are effectively unusable, which is the single biggest trust problem in the app right now.
tasks.technical_description: Row tables are already paginated to 100 (PAGE_SIZE=100), so raw DOM volume is not the cause. The prime suspect is unmemoized full-dataset work in the render path: InventoryBrowser.tsx:91-92 rebuilds the brand and vendor dropdown option lists with new Set(records.map(...)).filter().sort() over all ~12,811 records on every render. Wrap these (and any equivalent full-dataset derivations in VendorView and PurchaseOrders) in useMemo keyed on the source array. Profile with React DevTools to confirm the synchronous hot path, and check whether the freshness bar triggers a global inventory fetch/recompute on every route. Add list virtualization only if memoization alone is insufficient.
likely_files: app/src/pages/purchasing/InventoryBrowser.tsx:91-92 - brands/vendors option lists built over all records without useMemo
app/src/pages/purchasing/InventoryBrowser.tsx:58-80 - filtered/sorted useMemos to verify dependency arrays
app/src/pages/purchasing/VendorView.tsx + VendorView.helpers.ts - vendor grouping over full dataset
app/src/pages/purchasing/PurchaseOrders.tsx - profile the freeze on this page (only ~500 orders, so investigate detail/items loading and any global fetch)
app/src/components/layout/DataFreshnessBar.tsx - confirm it does not force a heavy recompute on every navigation
requirements: Memoize all full-dataset derivations (dropdown option lists, groupings, sort inputs) so they do not recompute every render.
Inventory Browser, Purchase Orders, and Vendor View must become interactive within a few seconds on the production dataset.
No functional regression to filtering, sorting, or pagination on those pages.
completion_criteria: All three pages render and respond to input in < ~3s on a ~12,811-row dataset (no multi-second main-thread block).
React DevTools shows the previously-hot computations no longer run on every render.
Existing helper tests still pass; add a guard test if a helper is refactored.
expected_outcomes: Inventory Browser, Purchase Orders, and Vendor View load quickly and stop wedging the tab.

unit: If option-list building is extracted to a helper, test it returns sorted unique non-empty values.
integration: Render InventoryBrowser with a large fixture and assert it mounts without throwing; optionally assert memo identity is stable across re-renders.
manual_qa: Navigate to Inventory Browser, Purchase Orders, and Vendor View in sequence; confirm none freezes the tab and all become interactive within seconds.

clarifying_questions: Is a memoization-first fix acceptable for this sprint, with list virtualization deferred to a follow-up if still needed, or do you want virtualization included now?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying question and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: FEATURE: Extract InboundPipeline helpers and add test coverage (sort, filter, arrival-month grouping)
tasks.outline: InboundPipeline currently has all its sort/filter/arrival-month-grouping logic inline in the .tsx with zero automated tests, which is exactly why the MVP arrival-month/ETA verification can't be confidently signed off. Extracting helpers and testing them locks in the pagination fix from task 1 and the month-grouping behavior.
tasks.technical_description: Pull the inline derivations out of app/src/pages/purchasing/InboundPipeline.tsx into a new app/src/pages/purchasing/InboundPipeline.helpers.ts (sku/vendor/expectedDate accessors, filter predicate, sortValue/sort, and the expected-delivery-month bucketing including the 'No ETA' bucket and the contiguous-month-range fill). Mirror the helpers+test pattern used by VendorView and InventoryBrowser. Add app/src/__tests__/InboundPipeline.helpers.test.ts.
likely_files: app/src/pages/purchasing/InboundPipeline.tsx:66-101,121-149 - accessors, filter, sortValue to extract
app/src/pages/purchasing/InboundPipeline.tsx:262-280 - month-grouping feeding the chart
app/src/pages/purchasing/VendorView.helpers.ts - pattern to follow
app/src/__tests__/VendorView.helpers.test.ts - test style to follow
requirements: Move sort/filter/arrival-month grouping into InboundPipeline.helpers.ts with no behavior change.
Cover the month bucketing: normal months, the 'No ETA' bucket, and the empty-month-in-range behavior (regression of prior bug 1214683866413708).
InboundPipeline.tsx imports the helpers; UI output is unchanged.
completion_criteria: InboundPipeline.helpers.test.ts exists and passes.
Arrival-month grouping is verified by test for in-range empty months and null ETAs.
cd app && npm test is green.
expected_outcomes: The Inbound arrival-month/ETA logic is testable and regression-protected, unblocking verification task 1214539638231259.

unit: InboundPipeline.helpers.test.ts: month bucketing, No-ETA handling, filter predicate, sort ordering.
manual_qa: Confirm the Inbound chart and table look identical before/after the refactor.

clarifying_questions: Should the month axis bridge far-outlier past months (e.g. a lone Jun 2025) with empty buckets, or clamp old outliers into a single bucket? (Tie-breaks the minor axis-gap finding from QA.)
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying question and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

This task was already In Progress and the audit confirmed the code exists but the test does not.

Cover downloadCsv filename + MIME with mocked Blob/URL APIs.
Tests run under the existing vitest config.

cd app && npm test is green.

tasks.title: FEATURE: Add a link to the PO on the Inbound Pipeline
tasks.outline: On Inbound Pipeline the PO number renders as plain text, so users can't jump from an inbound line to its purchase order. Linking the PO column to the Purchase Orders view closes the loop between inbound exposure and the underlying PO.
tasks.technical_description: InboundPipeline.tsx already imports Link from react-router-dom and renders item.po_id in the PO column. Wrap po_id in a Link to the Purchase Orders page targeting that PO (e.g. /purchasing/purchase-orders?po=<po_id>) and teach PurchaseOrders.tsx to read a ?po= URL param to pre-open/pre-filter that PO's detail (PurchaseOrders already has usePurchaseOrderItems(poId) and a detail modal). Follow the URL-param-driven filter pattern documented in CLAUDE.md (useLocation + URLSearchParams).
likely_files: app/src/pages/purchasing/InboundPipeline.tsx:2 (Link import), PO column cell rendering item.po_id
app/src/pages/purchasing/PurchaseOrders.tsx:68,228 - usePurchaseOrders + usePurchaseOrderItems detail modal to open from ?po=
app/src/pages/purchasing/InventoryBrowser.tsx - reference implementation of URL-param-driven pre-filtering
requirements: PO number on Inbound is a clickable link to the Purchase Orders view for that PO.
Purchase Orders reads the ?po= param and opens/filters to that PO.
Respect role access (admin + purchasing only) and degrade gracefully if the PO isn't cached.
completion_criteria: Clicking a PO on Inbound lands on Purchase Orders showing that PO.
Direct navigation to /purchasing/purchase-orders?po=<id> opens the same view.
No console errors; csuite cannot reach the route.
expected_outcomes: Users move from an inbound line straight to its PO in one click.

unit: Helper/test for building the PO link href from po_id.
integration: PurchaseOrders honors ?po= and opens the matching PO detail.
manual_qa: From Inbound, click a PO number; confirm it opens that PO on Purchase Orders.

clarifying_questions: Should the PO link open the PO detail modal directly, or just filter the Purchase Orders list to that PO?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying question and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: FEATURE: Day-view DoD breakdown - what wasn't done yesterday and what changed
tasks.outline: The Today view shows today's tasks and a 'Completed yesterday' count but not what was left undone yesterday or how the day's picture changed day-over-day. Adding a DoD (day-over-day) breakdown gives the team a morning view of carryover work and movement.
tasks.technical_description: Extend app/src/pages/work/TodayView.tsx and TodayView.helpers.ts. Today's helpers already compute counters and partitions (computeTodayCounters, partitionTodayTasks). Add a DoD computation: tasks due/assigned yesterday that were not completed (carryover), plus deltas (created vs completed vs still-open day-over-day). Render a new 'Carried over from yesterday' section and/or a small DoD summary strip, reusing KPICard and the existing task-card renderers. Keep other users' tasks inspect-only per the existing Today rules.
likely_files: app/src/pages/work/TodayView.tsx:135-178 - counters strip + sections to extend
app/src/pages/work/TodayView.helpers.ts - add carryover/DoD computation next to computeTodayCounters/partitionTodayTasks
app/src/__tests__/TodayView.helpers.test.ts - extend with DoD cases
app/src/hooks/useTasks.ts - task fields (status, due_date, completed timestamps) feeding the calc
requirements: Compute yesterday's not-completed (carryover) tasks and a day-over-day summary.
Surface carryover as its own section and/or DoD summary on the Today view.
Reuse existing task-card rendering and respect inspect-only rules for other users' tasks.
completion_criteria: Today view shows what was not done yesterday and a clear DoD summary.
TodayView.helpers.test.ts covers carryover and DoD math.
Empty states render cleanly when there is no carryover.
expected_outcomes: The team opens Today and immediately sees unfinished work from yesterday and how things moved.

unit: TodayView.helpers.test.ts: carryover detection, DoD deltas, empty-carryover case.
integration: Render Today with a fixture spanning yesterday/today; assert carryover section + summary.
manual_qa: Open /today; confirm the DoD breakdown and carryover section are accurate against the task list.

clarifying_questions: What exactly counts as 'not done yesterday' - tasks with a yesterday due date still open, or any incomplete task touched yesterday? And should DoD be team-wide or per-user?
Note: backlog task 1209588240582952 ('Dod insight') overlaps with this; should it be merged into this task or kept separate?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: FEATURE: North Star page (monthly North Stars + weekly constraint/move + status)
tasks.outline: A new executive North Star page that anchors the team on a small set of leadership-set goals. Four North Star boxes are set once a month (admin-only edit once set), plus a weekly 'Constraint now' and 'This week's move' text box and an On track / Off plan status. Editing is inline (hover reveals an edit affordance) and every prior value is archived to the DB so the team can review history and run check-ins over time.
tasks.technical_description: New page + route, new Supabase tables with admin-edit RLS, and an inline edit-and-archive pattern. LAYOUT/VISUAL SPEC comes from two attachments on the Asana task that the implementer must follow: image.png (design mockup) and BPR-template.html (layout/tech-doc template - likely a Business Performance Review layout the page should adopt). Data model: a north_star table for the 4 monthly targets (slot index, value text, period_month, status enum on_track/off_plan, set_by, set_at) and a weekly_focus table for 'Constraint now' and 'This week's move' (value text, period_week, set_by, set_at); plus an append-only history table (or soft-versioning via period + superseded_at) so editing archives the previous value rather than overwriting - the description explicitly wants old values retained 'so we could check in in future'. RLS: all authenticated users read; only admin can insert/update (mirror the admin-only mutation pattern used by automation_config / set_default_auto_assignee). Add a migration under supabase/migrations/. UI: NorthStar.tsx page + NorthStar.helpers.ts, a new route in App.tsx wrapped in RoleGuard (read for all roles, edit gated to admin), and a sidebar link (C-SUITE section, near Executive Summary). Inline editing: hover shows an edit button; clicking opens an editor (reuse the Modal / TaskActionNoteModal interaction style) that writes the new value and archives the previous one; status is a dropdown (On track / Off plan). Cadence guardrails: North Stars are editable once per month, weekly fields once per week - enforce in the hook/DB.
design_inputs: Asana attachment image.png (gid 1215784652029229) - design mockup of the North Star page.
Asana attachment BPR-template.html (gid 1215798685000042) - layout/tech-doc template the page structure must follow.
NOTE: both attachments could not be opened during planning (Chrome extension was disconnected); their specifics must be reviewed and reflected before/while implementing - see clarifying_questions.
likely_files: app/src/App.tsx - add the /executive/north-star (or /north-star) route under RoleGuard
app/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx - add the North Star nav link
app/src/pages/csuite/NorthStar.tsx (new) + NorthStar.helpers.ts (new) - page + logic
app/src/hooks/useNorthStar.ts (new) - read + admin-only update/archive mutations (follow useTasks/useDismissedActions patterns)
supabase/migrations/ (new migration) - north_star + weekly_focus + history tables with admin-edit RLS
app/src/components/ui/Modal.tsx + components/tasks/TaskActionNoteModal.tsx - inline editor interaction patterns to reuse
app/src/types/index.ts - add NorthStar / WeeklyFocus types
requirements: Page renders 4 monthly North Star boxes, a 'Constraint now' weekly box, a 'This week's move' weekly box, and an On track / Off plan status dropdown.
Layout follows the BPR-template.html template and the image.png mockup attached to the Asana task.
Only admin users can edit; non-admins see read-only values.
North Stars are set once per month; the weekly fields are updated weekly.
Editing archives the previous value to the DB (history retained for future check-ins) before saving the new value.
Hover reveals the edit affordance; saving persists via Supabase with the correct RLS.
Add a migration; all reads paginate if a list ever exceeds the Supabase cap (per CLAUDE.md convention).
completion_criteria: Admin can set/edit the 4 North Stars, both weekly fields, and status; non-admin cannot edit.
Each edit writes a new value and preserves the prior value in history (verifiable in the DB).
Monthly/weekly cadence is enforced (no second monthly edit within the same month, etc.).
Page layout matches the mockup + BPR template.
Helper/unit tests cover the cadence + archive logic; cd app && npm test is green.
expected_outcomes: Leadership sets monthly North Stars and a weekly constraint/move that the whole team sees on one page, with a retained history for check-ins.

unit: NorthStar.helpers.test.ts: monthly-vs-weekly cadence gating, archive-on-edit, status enum handling.
integration: Admin edit writes new value + archives prior; non-admin sees read-only and cannot mutate (RLS).
manual_qa: As admin: set all 4 North Stars, the weekly constraint, this week's move, and status; edit one and confirm the prior value is retained.
As a non-admin (purchasing/csuite): confirm the page is read-only.

clarifying_questions: Please confirm the layout details from BPR-template.html and image.png (sections, ordering, styling) - these attachments could not be opened during planning; reconnecting Chrome will let them be folded in precisely.
Where should the page live in nav - under C-SUITE (Executive) for csuite+admin, or visible to all roles read-only?
Confirm the history model: a separate append-only history table vs. period-based versioning with a superseded_at timestamp.
Should the monthly/weekly cadence be a hard lock (cannot edit again until next period) or a soft warning that still allows admin override?

tasks.title: FEATURE: Monthly Star section on the North Star page - sales goal and progress
tasks.depends_on: 1215784652029226 (North Star page - task 7); build after the page shell exists.
tasks.outline: A 'Monthly Star' section on the North Star page that tracks the monthly sales goal and live progress toward it. It shows the monthly total-sales target, month-to-date actual vs last year (YoY), the daily pace and pace-projected month-end, the goal gap (remaining dollars, remaining days, and the $/day and % lift needed to hit target), an on-track / not-on-track verdict, and the channels dragging performance.
tasks.technical_description: This is the sales-goal block of the North Star page (task 7). Render the metrics from the task description: monthly Total Sales target (e.g. $9,000,000); MTD actual vs LY with YoY % and $ delta; daily pace ($/day) and projected full-month total at that pace; goal gap = (target - projected or target - MTD) over remaining days, expressed as $/day needed and the % lift vs current pace; a status verdict (On track / Not on track) with the projected finish vs target; and a 'Drag' list of channels with negative contribution (e.g. FBA, DropshipCentral, WFS). Most of these are pure derivations from a few inputs (target, MTD actual, LY MTD, days elapsed, days remaining, per-channel deltas), so put the math in a NorthStar.helpers.ts / monthlyStar.helpers.ts and unit-test it hard - this is a money surface. KEY UNKNOWN: the data source for sales actuals, last-year comparison, and per-channel (FBA/WFS/DropshipCentral) breakdown is not in the current inventory CSV; confirm whether it comes from MySQL/SellerCloud metrics, a new upload, or a manual admin entry (see clarifying_questions). Visual placement follows the same North Star mockup (image.png) / BPR-template.html layout referenced in task 7.
likely_files: app/src/pages/csuite/NorthStar.tsx (new in task 7) - add the Monthly Star section
app/src/pages/csuite/NorthStar.helpers.ts (or monthlyStar.helpers.ts) - pace/gap/projection/YoY math
app/src/hooks/ - hook for sales actuals + LY + per-channel data (source TBD)
app/src/lib/financialMetrics.ts - reuse currency/number formatting and any existing sales aggregates
app/src/lib/utils.ts - fmtCurrency / fmtNumber
app/src/__tests__/ - new helper test for the projection/gap math
requirements: Show monthly Total Sales target, MTD actual, and LY MTD with YoY % and $ delta.
Compute and show daily pace, pace-projected month-end total, and the goal gap as $/day needed plus % lift.
Show an On track / Not on track verdict based on projected finish vs target.
List the channels dragging performance with their negative $ contribution.
Match the North Star page layout (mockup + BPR template) and live inside the North Star page (task 7).
All projection/gap/YoY math lives in a tested helper.
completion_criteria: The Monthly Star section renders all metrics from the spec with correct math for a known fixture.
Status verdict flips correctly around the target threshold.
Helper unit tests cover pace, projection, gap, YoY, and the on-track/off-track boundary; cd app && npm test is green.
Numbers reconcile with the data source agreed in the clarifying questions.
expected_outcomes: Leadership sees, at a glance on the North Star page, whether the month's sales are on pace to hit the monthly goal and what daily lift is needed to close the gap.

unit: monthlyStar.helpers.test.ts: daily pace, projected month-end, goal-gap $/day and % lift, YoY %, on-track vs not-on-track boundary, channel-drag sorting.
integration: Render the Monthly Star section with a fixture and assert the displayed target/MTD/pace/gap/status/drag values.
manual_qa: Open the North Star page; confirm the Monthly Star section shows target, MTD vs LY, pace, projected finish, goal gap, status, and channel drag, and that the verdict matches the numbers.

clarifying_questions: Where do sales actuals, the last-year comparison, and the per-channel (FBA / WFS / DropshipCentral) breakdown come from - MySQL/SellerCloud, a new upload, or manual admin entry? This determines whether a new hook/Edge Function is needed.
Is the monthly Total Sales target ($9M in the example) set manually by an admin (like the North Stars) or pulled from a system?
Should 'projected finish' use the simple current daily pace, or a smarter projection (e.g. weighting recent days / excluding weekends)?
Is this section admin-editable for the target only, with the rest read-only computed values?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Build on the North Star page from task 7. Confirm the sales data source and the target-setting model (clarifying questions) before writing code.

sprint_close_checklist: All sprint tasks marked completed in Asana with a comment citing file paths and line numbers.
All new tests pass: cd app && npm test.
Run cd app && npm run dev and smoke-test Inbound, Inventory Browser, Purchase Orders, Vendor View, Today, and the North Star page end-to-end.
Re-verify the two carryover In Progress tasks (1214539638231259, 1214539638231255) and close them if the bugs are resolved.
Update CLAUDE.md if any new module or convention was added (e.g. InboundPipeline.helpers.ts, NorthStar page + tables).
Update memory (project_sanders_intelligence.md) if the architecture changed.
